methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Tenstorrent, a Series D-stage chip design company, carries a current post-money valuation of $2.7B and has raised over $1B to date. Valuation benchmarking draws on a universe of 10 same-sector peers spanning both private and public markets, with public comparables including ALAB, NVDA, and AMD.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier from Series D forward, implies a valuation of $4.0B. Peer-median anchoring across 10 semiconductor-focused companies at similar valuation tiers yields $2.2B; the peer set ranges from $1.0B to $7.0B and includes SambaNova Systems ($5.0B), Cerebras, and PsiQuantum ($7.0B). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied because Tenstorrent's revenue is not disclosed. The sector median P/S across three public semiconductors stands at 25.3x, but lacks a revenue input.

These two methods support a triangulated range of $1.8B to $4.7B, with a median of $3.1B. The current $2.7B valuation sits within this band but toward the lower quartile. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory (unavailable for private-company assessment), path to profitability in a capital-intensive sector, and relative competitive positioning against the heterogeneous peer set spanning quantum, analog, and AI-specific design approaches.
